title: An interface's 'I' prefix should be followed by an uppercase letter
messageFormat: Rename interface '{0}'; the 'I' prefix should be followed by an uppercase letter.
summary: The interface convention treats the leading I as a one-letter prefix in front of a PascalCase name, so the second character should be uppercase. A name where the I runs straight into a lowercase letter reads as an ordinary word that merely starts with I, which defeats the prefix and misleads the reader.

seeAlso.text: Names of classes, structs, and interfaces
